claim: Confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements full boundary audit with cryptographic snapshot comparison, creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers on violations, and blocks PRD readiness when agents mutate project files.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() function creates SHA256 digests of all project files using git ls-files or filesystem walking
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that captures cryptographic project state

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps to detect added/modified/deleted files
evidence.why: Cryptographic comparison logic that detects any file mutations

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore snapshot captured before agent loop starts
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary state is recorded before any agent execution

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter snapshot captured after agents complete, diffed against before, stored in result.boundary
evidence.why: Evidence that post-execution boundary audit happens and results are captured

evidence.summary: Boundary blockers added to readiness.blockers array with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION code, prd_ready forced to false when blockers exist
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations actively block PRD readiness
Evidence that policy explicitly forbids project mutation

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files'
evidence.why: Evidence that agents are explicitly instructed to respect boundary

evidence.summary: Test verifies writeFileSync during agent execution creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker and sets status to blocked
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary audit detects mutations and blocks readiness

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates evidence agent results and checks blockers before allowing PRD progression
evidence.why: Evidence that PRD readiness validation enforces evidence requirements

risks: If git ls-files fails or filesystem walker misses files, boundary detection could be incomplete
If SHA256 collisions occur (extremely unlikely), mutations could go undetected
Race conditions between snapshot and agent execution could theoretically miss mutations if timing is exact

result.notes: Independently verified all explorer claims: boundary audit is fully implemented with cryptographic snapshot comparison, explicit enforcement via blockers that prevent PRD readiness, and test coverage confirms the system detects mutations and blocks readiness as required.
